title: Local stars — per-occurrence, localStorage
description: Star/unstar per OCCURRENCE (not per item — starring Registration must not star all 5 slots). Store in localStorage keyed on code+start. Optimistic UI. This is the pre-backend solo version; M2 syncs to crew state.

title: Schedule tab: day tabs, time grouping, search (title/abstract/track/room)
description: Render baked schedule.json: SAT/etc day tabs (America/Edmonton), grouped by time with 'now' separator, event rows (time, name, room). Search over title/abstract/track/room only (NO speaker data exists — persons/speakers empty). Roster-as-filter row (wrapping, never side-scroll) can come with crew (M2); solo build shows All. This is the thin thing that beats the official site.

title: a11y baseline (src/app/a11y.css) — imported first, never overridden
description: rem units throughout; text-size scale driven by the discrete slider (lower default M); AAA (7:1) contrast tokens both themes; visible focus rings; 44px min targets; prefers-reduced-motion; semantic HTML (button/nav/headings/time datetime); no maximum-scale/user-scalable=no; reflow at 400% with no horizontal scroll (map exempt via List peer). Built in from the first commit.

title: fetch-schedule.mjs: pretalx join + occurrence expansion + assertions
description: scripts\fetch-schedule.mjs fetches both pretalx endpoints, joins on code, EXPANDS occurrences (208 slots vs 178 unique codes — key occurrence ids on code+start timestamp, NOT index). Normalize localized strings ({en;..} or str). All day-grouping America\Edmonton. Synthesize ids for the 4 code-less Overflow entries. Writes committed src\data\schedule.json. Assertions; 208 slots \ 178 codes \ 4 days; Registration->5 occurrences; CZKVLN->4; ids stable across two runs even if a slot is removed. Depends on nothing; everything downstream depends on this shape.

title: Scaffold: Vite + Preact, TypeScript (strict), plain CSS
description: Project skeleton: Vite + Preact + TypeScript (strict tsconfig), plain CSS (no CSS-in-JS), no date lib (Intl.DateTimeFormat suffices — no DST during con). Add @cloudflare/workers-types for the later Worker/DO. Establish src/ layout (src/app, src/data, src/worker) and define branded ID types (OccurrenceId vs ItemCode) up front. Scripts run in TS via tsx. Step 1 of the first vertical slice.

title: [flag] Time-travel override ?now=... — build this FIRST
description: Build the ?now=2026-07-18T13:05:00-06:00 override before anything that reads "now". The con has not happened, so without it there is no way to test the bot digest, the map "on now" highlighting, or the ambient/panel split — three of the four things most likely to be quietly broken on day one. About 5 lines of code; not optional. It was in early drafts and got lost.
acceptance_criteria: ?now=2026-07-18T13:05:00-06:00 makes every now-dependent surface behave as if it were that instant.

title: M1 — Static SPA, no backend (browse/filter/search, local stars, import, .ics)
description: The ship-if-everything-else-fails milestone: pure static SPA, no server. Schedule browse/filter/search (title/abstract/track/room — NOT speaker, none exists), star locally, paste-import from fur-eh, and .ics download. Accessibility baseline is built in from the FIRST commit, not retrofitted: rem units everywhere with 16px floor, in-app text-size control (A/A+/A++, do not trust the Telegram webview), AAA 7:1 contrast both themes, 44x44px touch targets, semantic HTML, visible focus rings, prefers-reduced-motion, no maximum-scale / no user-scalable=no, reflow at 400% with no horizontal scroll. Opacity must never carry meaning. Schedule is the landing tab; the roster IS the filter (All pill + crew avatars, yours first, tap to union-filter). Free/busy shown as ring+text, never colour alone. Label people with no stars "no plans listed", never "FREE". Import: liberal parse, regex the six-char code and intersect against known codes; opens the real browser via WebApp.openLink (the Telegram in-app browser lacks your fur-eh login). One-time; the app then owns your stars.
design: Critical files: src/app/ics.js (client-only), src/app/a11y.css (text-size scale, contrast tokens, focus rings — imported first, never overridden).
acceptance_criteria: Beats the official site offline. Accessibility tested at real magnification: 400% reflow with no horizontal scroll, text-size control works, contrast measured >=7:1 in both themes, all controls 44px+ and reachable. Import round-trip: matched count equals star count on the official site. .ics imports on a real phone with Headless Lounge as 4 separate events.

title: M0 — Scaffold + fetch-schedule.mjs (occurrence expansion + assertions)
description: Foundational data + scaffolding. Set up Vite+Preact (plain CSS, no TS), and build scripts/fetch-schedule.mjs: fetch both pretalx endpoints, join on 'code', expand occurrences, write committed src/data/schedule.json. A pretalx 'code' is a submission, not a slot: 208 slots collapse to 178 unique codes (pretalx ICS shows this via -0/-1/-2 UID suffixes). Occurrence IDs MUST be stable across schedule updates — key on 'code'+start timestamp, NEVER on occurrence index (a cancelled slot renumbers everything after it). No speaker data exists (persons:[] / speakers:[]) — search covers title/abstract/track/room only. 4 sessions have no code (Overflow Seating) — synthesise id:<n>. Normalize localized strings ('Main Stage' or {en:'Main Stage'}). All day-grouping is America/Edmonton regardless of device tz.
design: Critical file: scripts/fetch-schedule.mjs (pretalx join + occurrence expansion — everything depends on this shape).
acceptance_criteria: npm run schedule asserts: 208 slots / 178 codes / 4 days; Registration -> 5 occurrences; CZKVLN -> 4. Occurrence IDs stable across two runs even if a slot is removed. Localized strings never render as [object Object].

title: Paste-import flow from fur-eh favourites
description: Can't be automatic (favourites endpoint sends ACAO:* without credentials — correct security). Link (real browser via openLink) to schedule while logged in, then to favourites JSON; 'select all, copy, paste'. Parse liberally: regex \b[A-Z0-9]{6}\b and intersect with known codes so over-matching is harmless. Confirm 'Found 34 — all 34 matched' + list titles. One-time; app owns stars after.

title: .ics export UI (uses ics.js from 4cz.1)
description: Me-tab 'Download .ics' button generates the file client-side from starred occurrences (server never sees it). Opt-in 'remind me 10 min before' checkbox adds VALARM (default OFF). Correctness lives in 4cz.1 (per-occurrence UIDs on code+start, 75-octet folding without splitting UTF-8, escape \;,+newlines, UTC Z, CRLF, DTSTAMP, METHOD:PUBLISH). Static snapshot, not live.

title: Text-size discrete slider + split Display into Text size / Theme
description: Telegram does NOT reliably forward OS text scaling (iOS WKWebView ignores Dynamic Type; WebApp API has no font field) — so the in-app control is load-bearing. Replace the single 'Display' block with two labelled sections: Text size (discrete slider, fixed stops S/M/L/XL/XXL mapping to root font-size, LOWER default ~M so we stop over-indexing on a big minimum, range above old A++, persisted) and Theme (Dark/Light). rem-based CSS scales everything from root. Optional :root{font:-apple-system-body} as iOS progressive enhancement only.

title: Underground-floor georeferencing via shared circulation cores
description: Wyndham P1/P2 are underground (confirmed w/ hotel) — no OSM footprint. Align each to the Main Floor by matching the shared elevator (opposite Front Desk) + stairs (bottom-right) icons as 2 control points (similarity transform: translation+rotation+uniform scale). Main Floor anchors to OSM. Generalizes: Delta 2nd -> Delta Main the same way; only one floor per building needs an OSM anchor. Store the 2 control-point coords per floor with the polygons for reproducibility.

title: List view = alphabetical text-map (not the old Now tab)
description: A text rendering of the same spatial data, driven by the shared building/floor filter. Room header (A-Z), current panel (or none/ambient with next open-close), any crew present with signal named (GPS / star / GPS+star agree) and staleness. Building-level view adds a MAIN/2ND floor badge per room. Faster than panning at high magnification.

title: Shared building/floor selector — no SITE pill, drives Map AND List
description: Single control, no SITE pill: empty selection = site. Select hotel -> its floor pills appear, Main auto-selected (or last). Other hotel stays as jump target. Wyndham P1/P2 labelled 'underground'. Selection maps to both views: none->OSM both hotels (map)/all rooms A-Z (list); building-only->OSM zoom of that hotel (map)/all its rooms A-Z (list); building+floor->drawn floorplan (map)/that floor's rooms A-Z (list). Custom geometry only shows when a floor is selected. OSM always renders outside buildings. Supersedes the earlier 'selector is Map-only' note.

title: OSM outdoor basemap — bake to SVG at build time
description: New scripts/build-basemap.mjs: Overpass (bbox ~250m around both hotels) -> osmtogeojson -> project via shared ENU transform -> SVG paths -> commit generated output. Pull highway/footway, amenity=parking, nearby building outlines, LRT. ZERO runtime tile/CDN requests. Same ENU transform as GPS so dots land exactly. ODbL: visible '© OpenStreetMap contributors' credit reachable on the Site view; keep Overpass query + GeoJSON in repo.

title: rooms.json from corrected QRG — all 21 rooms
description: Author src/data/rooms.json polygons by tracing the corrected static/FUREH2026_HOOM_QRG_WEB.pdf against OSM footprints. Delta 2nd = Fort McMurray, Red Deer, Medicine Hat, Edmonton, Canmore (Headless Lounge), Glacier, Lethbridge, Calgary, Grande Prairie. Wyndham Main (Terraces 1-5, Lounge, Atrium, Atrium Cafe, Sushi Toshi, Front Desk, entrances), Parking 1 (Imperial/Second Stage), Parking 2 (Jubilee/Main Stage, Galleries 1-3, Fitness/Pool, Con Ops). Delta Main (Grande Ballroom/Meowria, Crystal Gallery/Meowdor). No guessed pins; build gate (aau.1) enforces resolution.

title: [human] Where is Delta - Glacier Room? (13 sessions, on no floor plan)
description: NOT a coding task — needs a human decision, and the only unresolved gap in the entire plan. Delta-Glacier Room has 13 scheduled sessions but appears on no floor plan. Meanwhile the Delta plan shows Banff, Lake Louise, Jasper and Grande Prairie — all with ZERO sessions. A room was almost certainly renamed between the map going to print and the schedule being locked, but the PDF cannot tell us which one. Ask con staff, or someone who has been in the building. Until resolved it renders as "location unknown — ask at Registration", never a guessed pin (a confidently wrong map is worse than an incomplete one for wayfinding). Tracked separately and MUST NOT block M4 (fureh-schedules-aau) — the map ships with Glacier as a known, tracked gap.
acceptance_criteria: A human confirms which drawn-but-empty room (Banff / Lake Louise / Jasper / Grande Prairie) is actually Glacier, or confirms it is genuinely unmapped. Then rooms.json is updated to a real polygon or Glacier stays on the location-unknown list by decision.
close_reason: Resolved: original QRG PDF was wrong. Corrected static/FUREH2026_HOOM_QRG_WEB.pdf shows Glacier Room on Delta 2nd (between Canmore/Headless Lounge and Lethbridge). Banff/Lake Louise/Jasper do not exist. All 21 rooms now map.

title: [flag] Leave-flow "also cancel my events" checkbox MUST default unchecked
description: Leave (pure privacy: removes you from roster + map, no side effects on others) and Cancel (owner-only, shows [CANCELLED]) are separate jobs. The leave flow offers a checkbox "also cancel all my custom events". It MUST default UNCHECKED — pre-ticked, a privacy action would silently destroy other people plans by default (a textbook dark pattern, exactly the failure the leave/cancel split was designed to prevent).
acceptance_criteria: The checkbox is unchecked on open; leaving without ticking it leaves other members starred custom events intact.

title: [flag] Build gate: every schedule room resolves, or the build fails
description: Every one of the 21 schedule rooms must resolve to EITHER a polygon in rooms.json, the off-map allow-list (Delta-Downtown, Delta-Parking Garage), OR the explicit location-unknown list (currently Delta-Glacier Room). A room in none of the three FAILS the build — it never renders a ghost pin. Also handle unknown rooms at RUNTIME: fur-eh can rename a room mid-con (very likely what happened to Glacier) and live /api/schedule would then serve a room the map has never heard of — it must degrade to "location unknown", never throw.
acceptance_criteria: Adding an unmapped room to the feed fails the build; an unknown room from live /api/schedule degrades to "location unknown" at runtime without throwing.

title: [flag] Ambient-venue rule — 25 sessions >6h are venues, not panels
description: 25 sessions run over 6 hours (Dealer Den, Registration, Art Show, the lounges) — these are ambient venues, not panels. This rule lost its home when the Now tab was deleted. Re-apply it to the two surfaces that now answer "what is on": the bot digest (ambient venues go in a one-line footer, never the headline) and the map room highlighting (an always-open room is not "happening now"). Without it, the digest is permanently topped by "Registration is on", every day, forever. NOTE: the map-highlighting half of this rule is implemented in M4.
acceptance_criteria: The digest never leads with an ambient venue; ambient venues appear only in a footer line.
notes: REVISED: ambient venues also show their NEXT open/close transition, not 'open all day'. Before open -> 'Dealer''s Den: opens 9:00 AM'; during -> 'closes 6:00 PM'. Real hours from the QRG: Registration Thu 10-4:30 & 6:15-10 / Fri 10-10 / Sat 11:30-10 / Sun 11-1; Dealer''s Den Fri 10-6 / Sat 11-6 / Sun 10-4; Art Show Thu 6:30-8:30 / Fri 10-5 / Sat 11-5 / Sun 10-1:30. Applies to bot digest footer + any app ambient labels. Digest must stay within Telegram's inline-HTML subset (b/i/u/s/a/code/pre/blockquote only).

title: [flag] First-run consent notice — one-time, non-blocking
description: Deleting onboarding also deleted the only moment where anyone learns that their starred events are visible to everyone in the crew. As drafted, tapping the pinned link out of curiosity silently puts your name in the roster and your plans on the map — nobody consented to that. Fix: a one-time, non-blocking notice on first open — "You have joined <crew>. Your starred events are visible to this group. You can leave any time." One line and a Continue. Not a wizard, but not nothing either.
acceptance_criteria: The notice appears exactly once, is non-blocking, and is shown before a new member becomes visible to the crew.
notes: REVISED: consent screen must explain Ghost mode in 3 plain bullets (crew sees 'no plans listed'; you don't appear on the map; stars/.ics/schedule still work for you) — naming it is not consent. Non-blocking, one-time.

title: [flag] Expiry enforced in code — Durable Object storage has NO TTL
description: Durable Object SQLite has no expirationTtl (that was KV thinking carried into a DO design). Nothing expires unless we delete it. Enforce expiry inside the 5-minute alarm: purge stale loc every tick (older than ~20 min -> drop it; also treat it as absent on read so a missed tick cannot resurrect a stale dot). After the con + 60 days the DO deletes its own storage (deleteAll()) and stops re-arming its alarm. If this is not built, the "it all evaporates after the con" privacy promise is simply false. Rides the same alarm introduced in M3.
acceptance_criteria: Stale loc is purged by the alarm and treated as absent on read; the 60-day self-delete path exists.

title: [flag] .ics fold/escape + opt-in VALARM — write and test these functions FIRST
description: In src/app/ics.js: per-occurrence UIDs keyed on code + start time (NOT an index, or four days of Headless Lounge collapse into one event). Fold at 75 OCTETS, never splitting a UTF-8 sequence (custom events contain emoji). Escape backslash, semicolon, comma, and newlines. Unfolded or unescaped lines make Apple Calendar silently import NOTHING, with no error. UTC Z times, no VTIMEZONE, omit X-WR-TIMEZONE. CRLF endings, DTSTAMP on every event, METHOD:PUBLISH. Contradiction resolved: an earlier draft justified the file with "passive reminders" and then specified No VALARM (which disables reminders entirely). Fix: an opt-in "remind me 10 minutes before" checkbox on the download, default OFF, that adds a VALARM per event only when asked.
acceptance_criteria: RFC 5545 validator passes; the longest abstract folds without corrupting emoji; a VALARM is present only when the box is ticked (off by default).

title: M4 — Map: vector SVG floors + facilities, Site->Building->Floor, hotspots, List toggle, crew by stars
description: The centrepiece and the only home for "where are my people". Vector SVG drawn from scratch (NEVER raster — the primary user is legally blind at near-max magnification; a raster map cannot serve them). Draw in real-world metres: the OSM building footprint is the outer boundary of each floor (correct scale/rotation/position by construction), the QRG is a topology reference only, the SVG coordinate space is metres so lat/lng -> position is exact. Full facility set (washrooms, elevators, stairs, front desk, entrances, "to parkade", registration, cafes, lounges), not just panel rooms. Hierarchy Site->Building->Floor: Wyndham (Main/Parking1/Parking2) + Delta (Main/Second). 18 of 21 rooms mapped. Crew placed by stars (a star names the room -> the room names the floor, zero inference). The List toggle is a PEER view, not a fallback (panning a big vector map is slow at high magnification; a text list is often faster). Off-map allow-list: Delta-Downtown (off-site GoH dinner, in-app alert + maps link) and Delta-Parking Garage (Motorama). The map is exempt from the no-horizontal-scroll rule (a floor plan is irreducibly 2D; WCAG allows it — hence the List peer). Never render a guessed pin: unknown rooms show "location unknown — ask at Registration". Build-order note: M3 is sequenced before this but the map does not technically depend on the bot; it depends on crew state (M2) and room data (M0).
design: Critical files: src/data/rooms.json (room + facility polygons in real metres, labels, floor, building, off-map allow-list, location-unknown list); src/data/buildings.json (OSM footprints Wyndham way 321836536 / Delta way 321840654 + metres<->pixels origin). Does NOT depend on resolving Glacier Room — that is a separate human-decision issue and must not block this milestone.
acceptance_criteria: Every one of the 21 schedule rooms resolves to a polygon, the off-map allow-list, or the explicit location-unknown list. A room in none of the three FAILS the build (never renders a ghost). Map labels crisp at max zoom, AAA contrast in both themes. The List toggle shows the same data.
notes: REVISED (see docs/plan-revisions.md): (1) 21/21 rooms now map — Glacier resolved; rooms.json authored from corrected static/FUREH2026_HOOM_QRG_WEB.pdf. (2) OSM outdoor basemap folded in: bake OSM streets/parking/paths to SVG at build time, zero runtime tiles, ODbL attribution required. (3) Map nav = ONE shared building/floor control, NO SITE pill (empty selection = site); same control filters BOTH Map and List. (4) List view = alphabetical text-map (room header + current panel + crew present + signal + staleness). (5) Underground floors (Wyndham P1/P2) georeferenced to Main Floor via shared elevator+stairs control points; generalizes to Delta 2nd->Main. Only one floor per building needs an OSM anchor.

title: M3 — Bot: pinned "Happening Now" digest, DO-alarm-edited, repost fallback + auto-upgrade
description: Each crew Durable Object sets its own 5-minute alarm and editMessageTexts a single pinned "Happening Now" message (what is on, who is going, what is next, who is free, a button into the Mini App). Telegram edits fire no notification, so the digest updates all weekend silently and works even for people who never open the app. Use DO Alarms, NOT a global Cron Trigger (no KV means no crew registry to enumerate; cron caps at 250/account). DO Alarm gotchas: alarms are at-least-once with up to 6 retries — the alarm() handler MUST be idempotent (dedupe key in SQL) or a retry double-posts; alarms do not repeat, so re-arm at the START of the handler; one alarm per DO (setAlarm overwrites). Telegram webhooks need a fast 200 — write, return, and push slow work into ctx.waitUntil(). Pinning requires admin: if it cannot pin, repost a fresh digest every 30 min with one "make me an admin" nag line; on my_chat_member promotion the bot pins its next digest and switches to quiet-edit by itself (no config). A human must ADD the bot (Telegram forbids self-join) — make it a single tap via a deep link.
design: Critical file: src/worker/telegram.js (webhook, my_chat_member pin-upgrade, digest). meta.pinMode is "edit" or "repost".
acceptance_criteria: The pinned message edits in place and fires NO notification — including the initial pin (disable_notification: true on the pin call). Promote the bot mid-run and confirm it self-upgrades from repost to pin. A retried alarm does not double-post.

title: M2 — Worker + Durable Object: resolve + access codes, crew state, /api/schedule
description: Backend: Cloudflare Workers + Durable Objects (one per crew, each with its own Alarm) + Static Assets, on Workers Paid from day one. POST /api/resolve verifies Telegram initData (HMAC-SHA256, secret = HMAC(bot_token, "WebAppData") — argument order matters), mints an access code; every later request presents that code so Telegram and web users share one code path. The web client is first-class (random code stored in the browser). The crew is the group chat (the bot mints a crewId from chat_id, carried as a startapp param inside initData). initData carries first_name/username/photo_url so names and avatars just appear — onboarding is deleted. The DO holds all crew state (meta/members/events). Custom events are their own records, not nested inside a member. wrangler.jsonc MUST use new_sqlite_classes (NOT new_classes, which is KV-backed, fails on new accounts, and is unconvertible once deployed), the CREW DO binding, run_worker_first for /api/* and /telegram/webhook (else the SPA fallback swallows API routes), and NO cron trigger. Route via idFromName(crewId). Leave (DELETE /me) revokes the access code.
design: Critical files: wrangler.jsonc (run_worker_first + DO binding + new_sqlite_classes migration, no cron); src/worker/crew-do.js (all crew state); src/worker/telegram.js (initData HMAC). API surface: health, schedule, resolve, crew/:id, crew/:id/me, crew/:id/events, tg/webhook.
acceptance_criteria: /api/health returns JSON not HTML (the run_worker_first gotcha) — first smoke test after deploy. A tampered initData is rejected. Leaving revokes the access code so a stale code cannot keep writing. The deployed DO class uses SQLite storage.

title: ODbL attribution surface for baked OSM basemap
description: Visible, reachable '© OpenStreetMap contributors' (ODbL) credit — a corner label or info affordance on the Site view satisfies it; need not persist during pan/zoom. Keep the Overpass query + generated GeoJSON in the repo.

title: Cognitive accessibility pass
description: Plain language (no jargon); predictable/consistent layout; one obvious primary action per screen; always a way out (custom-event cancel is one); confirm destructive actions (cancel event, leave crew); no time pressure / prefers-reduced-motion; icons always paired with text labels; state shown redundantly (shape+text, never colour/opacity alone). Alphabetical List order is part of this.

title: Guest-of-Honour moved-venue alert lives on the event, not Crew page
description: Remove the GoH-dinner alert banner from the Crew page. Attach it to the GoH Dinner event's own listing in the schedule: moved to Delta Edmonton Downtown main ballroom (water damage), ticket still valid, menu unchanged, free transport; address + 'Open in Maps' link out (off-site, not on the con map).

title: Crew: 'Your Events', Edit-into-creation, Cancel at bottom w/ confirm
description: Rename 'Unofficial events' -> 'Your Events'. Drop the standalone cancel card. Room-party card gets an Edit button that reopens the create screen pre-filled; that screen has a 'Cancel event' button at the bottom gated by a 'you sure' confirmation, with real [CANCELLED]-to-starrers semantics (leave != cancel, per bgx.1).

title: Custom event: free-text location first, cancel/back out
description: Reorder the 'Where' field so the free-text location is PRIMARY and first; 'tap a spot on the map' is secondary/optional below it (most events are 'Rm 1412'). Add an explicit cancel/back (X in header) so a misclicked + FAB discards without saving. Cognitive-a11y: no dead-end forms.

title: Remove meaningless gold header label on Schedule + Map
description: Drop the gold top-right 'when' label (SAT · 67 / SITE / SAT 1:05) — duplicates day tabs + status bar and reads as noise. If a datum is genuinely useful (e.g. filtered count), fold it into the relevant control's own label. Keep meaningful ones (Crew '5 MEMBERS', Me name).

title: [flag] Ghost mode — one boolean, restore it
description: Ghost mode existed in the first design and silently fell out through the redesigns. Without it, joining the crew means being visible and the only way to opt out is to leave entirely — too coarse for a furry con, where being seen at a particular panel is exactly the thing some people cannot afford. Ghost mode: your stars still sync, your .ics still works, the map and schedule stay fully usable, but the crew sees you as "no plans listed". One boolean, roughly 15 minutes of work; plausibly the difference between "I will use this" and "I will not".
acceptance_criteria: With ghost mode on, the crew sees you as "no plans listed" while your stars and .ics keep working.

title: M6 — Live location: DM opt-in, solid pin vs hollow ring, accuracy circle, staleness
description: Ambitious and honest location fusion. Shared via Telegram Live Location in a private chat with the bot — Telegram own UI supplies consent, expiry (15min/1h/8h), the banner and the stop button; we build NO consent flow. The bot stays in default privacy mode (reads nothing in the group). Off by default, never nagged. Signal fusion: GPS + a star on this floor -> a SOLID FILLED pin in the room (GPS confirms the building, the star supplies the floor — what Telegram alone cannot do); GPS only -> a HOLLOW RING at its true georeferenced position on every floor of that building plus the accuracy circle (the legend explains the shape once, no per-dot labels); star only -> avatar in the starred room; two overlapping stars -> both rooms with a "?"; nothing -> no marker. GPS outside both building footprints -> render on the Site view, not any floor. Every marker shows staleness ("last seen 11m ago") — a phone sitting through a 50-minute panel is the worst case for freshness; a stale marker must look stale. Strokes are thick and fills are solid; the accuracy circle is a real filled area, not a 1px hairline. Shape carries uncertainty; opacity never does. If sockets ever become necessary they MUST use the Hibernation API — plain WebSockets keep the DO active 24/7 (~$22 overage). Uses 45s polling, stopped when not visible.
design: Critical file: src/data/buildings.json (georeferencing origin). Cut-order note: live location is the FIRST thing cut.
acceptance_criteria: Solid pin only when the GPS building matches a star floor; a hollow ring otherwise on all floors of that building with the accuracy circle. Off-site GPS renders on the Site view. Stale markers look stale and are treated as absent past ~20 min. No WebSockets (or Hibernation API only).

title: M7 — Theme polish (Lord of the Wings, light+dark) + privacy sweep
description: Ship-ready polish. Theme "Lord of the Wings" — fantasy in the chrome, NEVER in the content. Chrome (headers, tab bar, buttons, empty states, map border/compass, dividers, the bot message header): display serif, illuminated drop caps, gold rules, wing motif. Content (schedule rows, room labels, times, names): a boringly legible sans, high contrast, NO texture behind text ever. Light = ink on vellum (near-black on warm off-white, not beige — beige cannot hit 7:1). Dark = gold-on-deepwood using fur-eh green 3aa57c plus amber-gold accents on near-black. Privacy sweep: X-Robots-Tag noindex + a disallow-all robots.txt; no crew-list endpoint or cross-crew discovery; bot default privacy mode; live location opt-in / DM-only / ephemeral; no check-ins, chat, photos, or push; a no-referrer meta tag (the crew URL leaks in the Referer header to pretalx otherwise); zero third-party runtime requests (self-host the fonts); Telegram avatars proxied through the Worker not hotlinked (hotlinking leaks every viewer IP to Telegram on every load).
design: Ghost mode and the first-run consent notice are tracked as separate sub-tasks but land in this privacy sweep window.
acceptance_criteria: AAA 7:1 contrast in BOTH themes, no texture behind any body text. noindex + no-referrer + font self-host + avatar proxy all in place. No third-party runtime requests. Loads acceptably under Slow 4G (hotel wifi).

title: M5 — Custom events: create/cancel, tap-to-place, crew-visible, starrable, into .ics
description: Unofficial events the official schedule will never carry (room parties, dinners). The + opens: title, day, start/end, notes, and where. Tap-to-place on the map (you tap the floor plan on screen — nothing to do with being physically present; works days in advance); free text stays available; off-site things pin at the Site level. Guest rooms are not on the floor plans — a pin for "Rm 1412" is approximate, the room number in the title does the navigational work. Anyone can star a custom event; only the owner can edit or cancel it. Custom events are their own records (not nested in a member) — this is what makes leave-not-equal-cancel possible. Leave the crew (removes you from roster + map, pure privacy, no side effects on other people plans) is SEPARATE from Cancel this event (owner-only, shows [CANCELLED] to everyone who starred it rather than silently vanishing). The leave flow offers a checkbox "also cancel all my custom events".
design: Cut-order note: custom-event EDITING is cuttable (keep create + cancel); tap-to-place is cuttable (free text only).
acceptance_criteria: Leaving the crew must NOT remove a room party other people starred. The "also cancel my events" checkbox is UNCHECKED on open. Custom events flow into the .ics. Cancelled events show [CANCELLED] rather than vanishing.
